result:
```python
import re

def validate_signup(email, password, username):
    errors = []
    if not re.match(r'^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$', email):
        errors.append("invalid email format")
    if len(password) < 8:
        errors.append("password must be at least 8 characters")
    if not any(c.isdigit() for c in password):
        errors.append("password must contain a digit")
    if not re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9]{3,20}$', username):
        errors.append("username must be alphanumeric and 3-20 characters")
    return errors


if __name__ == "__main__":
    assert validate_signup("a@b.com", "pass1word", "alice123") == []
    assert "invalid email format" in validate_signup("notanemail", "pass1word", "alice")
    assert "password must be at least 8 characters" in validate_signup("a@b.com", "p1", "alice")
    assert "password must contain a digit" in validate_signup("a@b.com", "password", "alice")
    assert "username must be alphanumeric and 3-20 characters" in validate_signup("a@b.com", "pass1word", "a!")
    print("all checks pass")
```

Returns an empty list on success, or one or more strings naming each failed check. The `__main__` block self-tests the four failure paths and the happy path.
